text:
"The thing that comes for your kids when you're not paying attention" is such a perfect description. 

This story stands in for kids not being believed by mothers about step dad's abuse, not being listened to when they're truly threatened by bullies (would the parents be OK with regular assaults that no one did anything about? Hardly).

So many of King's books talk about how unseeing the adults are, which allows evil to run rampant, such as in "It".

The adults never notice that anything is even wrong, there's no huge response to so many missing kids.

Inattention is perhaps the biggest form of evil.

Ever since i was a child ive been afraid of the dark and have to sleep with the tv or sometimes a lamp on, closets CLOSED, bedroom door locked and a draft stopper covering the bottom of my door. "You'd wake up at three in the morning and look into the dark and at first you'd say, 'It's only the clock.' But underneath it you could hear something moving in a stealthy way. But not too stealthy, because it wanted you to hear it. A slimy sliding sound like something from the kitchen drain. Or a clicking sound, like claws being dragged lightly over the staircase banister. And you'd close your eyes, knowing that hearing it was bad, but if you saw it...
[...]

text:
Man this story might speak to me because as a kid I had full on hallucinations. From since I could remember to 12 years old I would always see these crazy situations. 

Like one time I woke up looking at the clock, being used to this and ready for it, usually at 3 am on the nose. And I looked to the foot of my bunk, I slept on the top bunk. And I saw a shadowy version of my sister hunched over standing on her knuckles looking at me. I flew off the top bunk landing on the ground with a loud thud, ran over and started flipping the light switch only for it to do nothing. Then assuming it must be burnt out I run light switch to light switch. I saw my sister in the hunched over position once more looking at me from the hallway behind, emotionless with black pits for eyes. I closed myself in the bathroom but it was darker, only a pale green light from the windows. Nothing else is turning on or changing. The time on the stove would be lit up and my alarm clock but only tiny lights like that. Motivated by the darkness inside the bathroom, I peaked, once I noticed she was gone, I ran all over the house trying to get the light to work but then I flipped my light switch in my room once more and everything lit up. The lights were on and I was flipping it on and off. My family was there but I couldnt see them the entire time. They didn't try to grab me or stop me at all it seems. Confused by what I was doing. The embarrassment ended up keeping me quiet about most of these situations. I should've seen someone for it but I never did. It ended up stopping once puberty started kicking in and I was becoming less afraid and more angry and annoyed. It stopped, one of the last things that happened I was playing hide and go seek with my brother, we had the house to ourselves so we were playing outside at night. At first it was inside with all the lights off. I counted and waited inside for him to hide somewhere on the block. I started to count to myself, out loud inside as he went out the front door. Pitch black besides my flashlight. As I counted I noticed how eerie it sounded, and started flashing my light off and on along with it. As I pointed it at the wall doing that, I looked at this knot in the wood pattern. It was eye like, the brown spiral of wood. The wood panel was kind of covered in these knots, but this one stood out. The brown swirls begin to twist and grow, until emerging from the wall was some kind of lanky tan humanoid. With large round yellow eyes with large black pits for pupils, and elongated face and elf like ears. I was stunned, immediately, my mouth opened and I struggled to find the words. It got closer, it's expression was impossible to determine, it's intentions, alien. All of it. My brain was short circuiting until I finally felt that fight or flight instinct, I was right next to the door I suddenly thought, and I left into the night extra motivated to find my brother. Sure enough, I did and as always by the time we returned it was gone. I was always isolated when it began, it didn't always end when someone else entered the situation though. I have had dozens of similar situations. It was such a relief to finally not be terrorized by them and it took years after to normalize. I was a strange kid until just about highschool because of it, for sure. Whatever caused the hallucinations was usually the time it was. Probably something to do with my dreams floating to the surface or something from not enough rem sleep.

This would have been one of the scariest American movies of all time and surpass the conjuring. This is the reason that Asian horror movies will always reign Supreme. There is no censorship, and they want to deliver an experience so that people all over the world can see. Hope this movie will at least be as scary as the conjuring. Stephen King and James wan are all we have left. Shyamalan lost his fire, and raimi isn't making horror movies like he used to anymore. Hopefully, Mr. King van pull off a miracle.
